messages.content: isn't it wild how a coffee that was like 2 bucks when i was a teenager is basically 6 now

Yeah, it really does hit different when you think about it that way! There's something about coffee prices specifically that feels personal because so many people buy it so regularly.

Though to be fair, inflation over the years does account for a decent chunk of that - like a dollar from 15-20 years ago genuinely bought more than it does now. But it still *feels* like a lot, especially when you're buying it multiple times a week and kind of doing the mental math.

The coffee shop experience has also changed a lot too - way more options, fancier equipment, higher labor costs in a lot of cities. But I get it, that doesn't make it sting less when you're just trying to get a normal cup of coffee, haha.

Do you still buy coffee out regularly or has the price pushed you more toward making it at home?
i was telling my coworker, feels like everything just quietly crept up and we all kinda accepted it

That's such a good way to put it - "quietly crept up." It really does happen gradually enough that there's no single moment where you go "okay THIS is too much," you just kind of... adjust and keep going.

And there's probably something to the fact that it happens across everything at once - coffee, groceries, rent, going out - so it's not like you can just swap to the cheaper alternative because the cheaper alternative also got more expensive.

It's almost like the frog in slowly heating water thing. Each individual price bump is small enough to absorb, but then you look back and think wait, when did a grocery run start costing THIS much?

I think a lot of people are feeling that same thing right now - that low key financial stress that's hard to even articulate because nothing dramatic happened, it just... shifted. Did your coworker feel the same way or were they more like "eh that's just how it goes"?
makes me a little nostalgic for the days when a 20 in your pocket felt like real money lol

Haha right?! A $20 used to feel like you were genuinely set for the day - coffee, lunch, maybe a little extra. Now it's like you pull it out and it's just... gone so fast.

There's something kind of bittersweet about that nostalgia too, because it's not just about the money - it's tied to a whole feeling of that time in your life, you know? Like simpler days in general, not just cheaper days.

Though I'm sure if you asked your teenage self, there were plenty of things to stress about back then too! It's funny how money stress kind of just shapeshifts as you get older rather than going away.

But yeah, $20 having real weight to it is a genuinely nice memory. What would a good $20 day have looked like for you back then?
anyway not complaining really, just one of those shower thoughts about how prices change over time

Haha yeah those shower thoughts about life and time can really sneak up on you! It's not even really complaining, it's just... noticing. Which is kind of interesting in itself.

Hope the rest of your Tuesday is good - you got much going on this afternoon?
